evidence.quote_or_summary: The Sandakphu trek can be done for about 8 months of the year barring only the rainy months of May to August.
Sandakphu is a great trek to do all 8 months of the year when it is open.

values: May
June
July
August

evidence.quote_or_summary: Through the monsoon season, from May to August the trek remains closed to visitors.

values: Spring: Rhododendrons and Magnolias in bloom along the trail
Autumn: Crisp air, clear mountain views, golden colours at sunrise/sunset, bird watching in the national park
Winter: Mild snowfall at Sandakphu and Phalut, colourful twilight skies, sunrise and sunset below the clouds, solitude on the trail

evidence.quote_or_summary: starting with the Rhododendrons and magnolias in the spring to crisp golden views of autumn and finally the mild snowy setting of winter
Red, pink Rhododendrons and white Magnolias dominate the landscape.
Autumn in Sandakphu is also fantastic for its golden colours, especially during the sunsets and sunrises.
This is also a great time to spot the varied varieties of birds in the national park.
the mild snowy setting of winter
Very few treks come close when we talk about skies at twilight.

values: January
February

evidence.quote_or_summary: You will find snow at Phalut and Sandakphu in the peak winter months of January and February.

values: Trek closed May to August due to monsoon
Mid-April onwards rains become more frequent, making the trek less enjoyable
Spring showers can cause fog and obscure long-distance peak views

evidence.quote_or_summary: Through the monsoon season, from May to August the trek remains closed to visitors.
By mid-April, the rains become more frequent. You might still be able to trek. But it’s not as much fun.
Occasional showers might turn the trail foggy or prevent the views of long-distance peaks and ranges.

values: Winter daytime: 8°C to 12°C; nighttime: 0°C to -3°C; 5 warm layers required
Spring daytime: 15°C to 20°C; nighttime: 1°C to 5°C; 4 warm layers required
Autumn daytime: 13°C to 18°C; nighttime: 5°C to 0°C; 4 warm layers required

evidence.quote_or_summary: Day time: Between 8 °C and 12 °C; Night time: Will drop to temperatures between 0 °C and -3 °C; No. of warm layers required: 5 warm layers
Day time: Between 15 °C and 20 °C; Night time: Will drop to temperatures between 1 °C and 5 °C; No. of warm layers required: 4 warm layers
Day time: Between 13 °C and 18 °C; Night time: Will drop to temperatures between 5 °C and 0 °C; No. of warm layers required: 4 warm layers

values: Winter: Some of the best twilight skies; sun rises and sets below the clouds
Spring: Views may be limited by fog and showers, but flowers compensate
Autumn: Clearest air and best long-distance peak views; golden sunrise/sunset colours

evidence.quote_or_summary: Some of the best twilight skies.
The sun rises and sets below you.
The flowers more than makeup for the lack of views owing to frequent showers that come down on the trail.
The air is clear and the view of peaks far away is amazing.
Mountain views are the best at this time.

values: Mid-April: increasing rain frequency reduces trek enjoyment
October: heavy crowds due to Durga Puja holidays and Land Rover tourists from Bengal
Winter: roads become difficult to drive on, reducing tourist numbers

evidence.quote_or_summary: By mid-April, the rains become more frequent. You might still be able to trek. But it’s not as much fun.
The Durga Puja holidays in October see a big number of tourists taking the Land Rovers and coming to Sandakphu.
Hardly any tourists as the roads become difficult to drive on and fewer vehicles on the trail.

values: Bird watching year-round (described as one of the Best Himalayan treks for Bird Watching)
Experiencing rhododendron and magnolia blooms in spring
Clear long-distance peak views and golden sunrise/sunset colours in autumn
Mild snow experience and solitude in winter
Colourful twilight skies in winter

evidence.quote_or_summary: It is one of the Best Himalayan treks for Bird Watching.
Rhododendrons and Magnolias are in full bloom. This is the best time to see the flowers.
Mountain views are the best at this time.
The good thing about snowfall at Sandakphu is that it is mild. Good enough to experience snow but not bad enough that the trail gets blocked.
there is a greater sense of solitude in winters

values: Mid-to-late April due to frequent rains
October for those seeking solitude, due to Durga Puja crowds

evidence.quote_or_summary: By mid-April, the rains become more frequent. You might still be able to trek. But it’s not as much fun.
The only flip side of trekking to Sandakphu in Autumn, especially October is the number of people on the trail.
